Ships look like actual classes

evidence_snippets: soft hum and ocean ambience
bring up an ambient sea layer

evidence_snippets: distinct splash for a miss
Distinct sound for miss
coverage_entries.reason: Miss sound (splash) explicitly described.

evidence_snippets: metal-on-metal thump for a hit
Distinct sound for hit
coverage_entries.reason: Hit sound (impact/thud) explicitly described.

evidence_snippets: creaking and water rushing
Distinct sound for sink
coverage_entries.reason: Sinking sound (creak/water) explicitly described.

evidence_snippets: 24x7 availability
game has to be running 24x7
coverage_entries.reason: Sources state requirement for 24x7 availability.

evidence_snippets: handle many simultaneous matches
scale horizontally
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require horizontal scale for many games.

evidence_snippets: 95 percent under 2 sec
tail should not exceed ~4 sec
coverage_entries.reason: Gold mentions "4%" but sources specify "4 seconds" tail.

evidence_snippets: no chat, no names, no personal info
coverage_entries.reason: Artifacts mandate anonymous presence and no PII.

evidence_snippets: stick to simple.
coverage_entries.reason: No measurable 9/10 no-training usability requirement present.

evidence_snippets: Language selection must happen at first access
coverage_entries.reason: Explicit requirement: English, Spanish, French and upfront choose.

evidence_snippets: Strict anonymity: no PII, no usernames, no avatars.
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple statements forbid revealing player identities.

evidence_snippets: Maintain a list of players present.
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes require keeping an anonymous player roster.

evidence_snippets: Players opt in to be shown.
coverage_entries.reason: Users can mark themselves available as players (opt-in).

evidence_snippets: both removed from lobby selection
coverage_entries.reason: When match starts, players are made unpickable immediately.

evidence_snippets: toggle off at any time
coverage_entries.reason: Users can remove themselves from availability immediately.

evidence_snippets: Inactive players should not be selectable.
coverage_entries.reason: Idle users are hidden from selection per notes.

evidence_snippets: After 24 hours of no heartbeat, remove
coverage_entries.reason: Docs specify removing users after 24 hours inactivity.

evidence_snippets: One-click start: select an available opponent
coverage_entries.reason: One-click lobby selection starts a match.

evidence_snippets: Two boards: offense and own defense grid
coverage_entries.reason: Each player shown their own defense grid.

evidence_snippets: offense tracking grid
coverage_entries.reason: Each player shown an offensive firing grid.

evidence_snippets: Opponent never sees either. No cross-board view.
coverage_entries.reason: Opponent is not allowed to view other's grids.

evidence_snippets: each player places five ships
coverage_entries.reason: Players place five ships on their defense grid.

evidence_snippets: horizontal, vertical, and also diagonal
coverage_entries.reason: Horizontal ship orientation explicitly allowed.

evidence_snippets: horizontal, vertical, and also diagonal
coverage_entries.reason: Vertical ship orientation explicitly allowed.

evidence_snippets: horizontal, vertical, and also diagonal
coverage_entries.reason: Diagonal straight-line placement explicitly allowed.

evidence_snippets: No overlap across ships. Reject placement if occupied
coverage_entries.reason: Overlapping ships are disallowed and blocked.

evidence_snippets: system decides who fires first
coverage_entries.reason: System assigns initial shooter status after placement.

evidence_snippets: one player is the shooter and the other is receiving fire
coverage_entries.reason: Explicit shooter vs receiver per turn stated.

evidence_snippets: Maintain a status per player: offensive or defensive
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes require per-player offensive/defensive status.

evidence_snippets: At the start of each turn, repeat that status.
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require turn-start notifications of status.

evidence_snippets: Shooter defines a shot by selecting a coordinate
coverage_entries.reason: Shooter selects a coordinate to define a shot.

evidence_snippets: system checks the target’s defense board
coverage_entries.reason: Server evaluates defender board to determine outcome.

evidence_snippets: System notifies both parties of outcome: hit or miss
coverage_entries.reason: Both players are informed of hit/miss outcomes.

evidence_snippets: On defender’s defensive grid: mark cell hit or miss
coverage_entries.reason: Defender's grid is explicitly marked with hit/miss.

evidence_snippets: On shooter’s offensive grid: mirror the same hit/miss mark
coverage_entries.reason: Attacker's firing grid mirrors hit/miss markings.

evidence_snippets: Notify both players that the ship is down
coverage_entries.reason: Sinking events produce notifications to both players.

evidence_snippets: defender board should show that ship as sunk
coverage_entries.reason: Defender's board displays sunk ship outline and type.

evidence_snippets: defender board should show that ship as sunk, labeled
coverage_entries.reason: Defender grid must show sunk ship type.

evidence_snippets: On shooter’s grid: indicate that ship is sunk as well.
coverage_entries.reason: Attacker's grid receives sunk indicator.

evidence_snippets: If hit: shooter immediately gets another shot.
coverage_entries.reason: Hit grants shooter another shot immediately.

evidence_snippets: If miss: change roles. Shooter becomes targeted
coverage_entries.reason: Miss causes offensive player to become defensive.

evidence_snippets: If miss: ... defender becomes shooter.
coverage_entries.reason: Miss causes defensive player to become offensive.

evidence_snippets: Continue assigning turns until end-of-game condition met.
coverage_entries.reason: Turns continue iteratively until game end.

evidence_snippets: Win condition: last remaining ship ... sinks. Immediately end match.
coverage_entries.reason: Game ends when last defender ship sinks.

evidence_snippets: either player can end a game at any time (resign)
coverage_entries.reason: Players may manually end/resign a match anytime.

evidence_snippets: When that happens, tell both players the match is over
coverage_entries.reason: Both players are notified when game ends.

evidence_snippets: Include reason: victory by all ships sunk, or opponent ended
coverage_entries.reason: End notifications include reason for conclusion.

evidence_snippets: present two actions: relist self as available
coverage_entries.reason: Post-match offers relist option.

evidence_snippets: Self removal instant.
coverage_entries.reason: Players can toggle off to remove immediately.

unsupported_implied.text: The system must provide atomic pairing/availability locking (e.g., compare-and-set) so that simultaneous selections of the same opponent cannot create multiple pairings.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Edge case: two people clicking same person. Need atomic lock. SRE to add compare-and-swap on availability flag.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly requires an atomic compare-and-swap style lock to prevent race conditions during pairing. The gold requirements describe maintaining a list, making players unavailable when a game is initiated, and allowing selection, but they do not require or imply an atomic server-side locking mechanism to prevent concurrent pairing races. This is a significant system-capability (concurrency control) not present in the gold set.

unsupported_implied.text: The server must be authoritative for turn/role state and confirmation of shot outcomes; clients must not locally award extra turns until the server confirms.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Tasha [DevOps SRE]: The roles state should be authoritative on the server. Clients can optimistically highlight, but do not award extra turns locally until the server confirms.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly mandates server-authoritative state for player roles and that clients must not grant extra turns locally before server confirmation. While the gold set requires maintaining and notifying player status per turn and marking hits/misses, it does not specify authority/location of truth (i.e., a server-side authoritative requirement) nor prohibition of client-side granting of extra turns. This architectural constraint is a significant capability beyond the gold requirements.
